items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Wow... are they all Echoes?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Yes. Most newcomers to the city are amazed by the spectacle.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Now we're talking! This is what the Land of Echoes should look like! I thought all of Rinascita's Echoes were like that grumpy poker face we met at the docks...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: ...Hmm?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Eh... Hi! I'm Abby. Nice to meet ya!

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Ah! Hello, I'm Phoebe! Nice to meet you, too.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Ah...

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: A little Echo holds up a gift and presents it to you.

options.text: They look different from the Echoes we just saw.
You don't like Echoes?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Yes, thanks to the Sentinel's blessing, most of the Common Echoes in Rinascita are friendly and approachable.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: It's not that... Members of the Order are instructed to maintain a distance from Echoes. We must stay objective to better share knowledge about them and honor our duty to inform the public.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: That Echo we saw is known as a Hocus Pocus. Children love its tricks, and they say that if an adult gets its gift, they can relive their happiest memories in dreams... ah...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: ...Sorry, I got carried away.

options.text: I think it sounds interesting.
Is that tower the Nexus?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: ...Yes. Allow me to introduce to you the Nexus of Ragunna, also known as the Genesis Nexus.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Yes, but the Ragunnesi prefer to call it the Genesis Nexus.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Input your personal information into the Nexus, and you'll be able to use all Common Echoes in the city just like a regular citizen.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Please place your terminal here.

items.speaker: Beacon Inscription
items.text: With might and justice, I rule from the throne, silencing ocean's tumult under my reign alone.

items.speaker: Beacon Inscription
items.text: For those who refuse to drown in swirling waves, heed this doctrine: Seek salvation from where all began.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Are you okay?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The Vitreum Dancer suddenly lost control... Luckily, no one got hurt.

options.text: Actually...

items.text: You brief her on what you just saw.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Now that I think about it, the frequency vibration I sensed felt a lot like... empathy.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Empathy…?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Well, maybe telepathy? Not sure. It's hard to explain…

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Telepathy… Yes, according to legend, the Sentinel speaks directly into the mind of the Laureate. Could that be what happened?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: So the "Imperator" is the Sentinel. But who's the "Lost"? Plus, it said it needs your help, but… a Sentinel shouldn't need to ask for help, wouldn't ya say? Eh, something's not adding up, what a headache...

options.text: We need to learn how the Sentinel talks to people.
That Vitreum Dancer was trying to tell me something…

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The Sentinel has countless ways to send a message, and through the Order is apparently the most convenient one, but why did they go through all the trouble...?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Maybe I'm wrong. Maybe calling for help was the only option?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (With that message left on Tethys… Could the Sentinel here be in a situation similar to Jué's?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But it seems like the locals haven't laid eyes on their Sentinel. Everything feels off. That Echo might be our only lead right now.)

options.text: Was the Echo performance hosted by the Montellis?
I want to find out what caused that Echo to lose control.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yes, it was. The Montelli family has been promoting the use of Reserved Terminals and Echoes.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: As far as I know, the Montelli family has been promoting the use of Reserved Terminals and Echoes.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Most Ragunnesi use Common Echoes and don't know much about the Reserved ones. The Montellis spotted a chance and started researching Reserved Terminals.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: They push to bring back the Carnevale so they can showcase their Reserved Terminals, prove the quality of their Echoes, and attract both customers and investors.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Oh sorry, just a minute.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yes, Mr. Alberto. An Echo was...

items.speaker: Unknown (83)
items.text: Zani recounts what happened to the caller. Moments later...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: ...I see.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Sorry to keep you waiting. That was Mr. Alberto from the Montelli family. Also my employer.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: He extends a sincere apology for what just happened. They too are at a loss to explain what caused the accident.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: He told me that the Montelli family would investigate further. Please rest assured.

options.text: Did the Montellis really have no answers?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: No... After all, such an incident only hinders their promotion of Reserved Terminals. They have no reason to rejoice over it.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Come to think of it, that Echo... it was looking at you. It seemed to target you.

options.text: You've got sharp eyes.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hmph, I won't pry into my ward's privacy. I only remember what I need to get the job done. Anything else I accidentally overhear just slips my mind.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: My orders are to guard and to guide. I do my job. Nothing more, nothing less. So if you want to investigate, I'll stick with you until you're done.

options.text: Thank you.
You're very professional!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Don't thank me, thank the family who hired me. Their generosity is beyond what you can imagine.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thank you for trusting in my abilities.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, what's our next move?

options.text: Let's find that Vitreum Dancer first.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It was complete chaos, but I spied the direction it fled… We can try our luck at the Pioneer Association.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I know someone there whose Forte might help us uncover some details we missed.

options.text: Sure, let's head to the Pioneer Association first.
Well, I think we'd better work the scene first.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Good. Let's go this way. It's right over there.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: No problem.

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: Has the perfume, compact, and jewelry set arrived?
Yes, they have. Would you like them now, or shall we deliver them to your residence before tonight?

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: Tonight… No, I'll take them now.
…Of course. I'll have someone fetch them immediately. It won't take long.
Welcome to Masked Reverie. From masks to elegant garments and splendid stage props, we craft it all to order. How may I help you today, my dear?

options.text: ...Who's there?

items.text: Hehe, so eager to skip the pleasantries, are we? I'm right here. Do I seem invisible, or are you simply ignoring me? I am... right behind you.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Madame Nyarla, I saw you. Your mask gave you away.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Hehe, don't spoil my secret so soon. It's not every day I get such a charming visitor.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Besides, only by "hiding" oneself can the true beauty of a mask be revealed, don't you think?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But scaring the guests is just your twisted sense of humor, isn't it?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I hope this didn't bother you. <ano=Ombra Elusiva>Invisibility</ano> is Madame Nyarla's Forte. Madame Nyarla, this is the distinguished guest invited by the Montellis, {PlayerName}. {Male=He;Female=She} is very interested in Carnevale masks.

options.text: Yes, I'd like a custom-made mask.

items.text: You elaborate on the mask design you saw in Fulmine's memory to Nyarla.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Hmm? That's quite a detailed description, even covering the smallest intricacies... I admit, I'm surprised.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Not long ago, we customized a mask just like the one you've described.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: If the Carnevale is a dream shared by all Ragunnesi, then a well-crafted mask is the key to it.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: When faces are hidden, souls break free from the shackles of everyday life. That's why we love balls and plays, eagerly awaiting the Carnevale.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Masks liberate our spirits. We believe they reveal what lies within. It is our honor to help our customers express their innermost selves.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: So, are you certain you want to custom order this particular mask?

options.text: Well…
(She's suspicious... Will I be forced to reveal my hand...?)

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: That's a very fine mask.

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: If I remember correctly, the one Mr. Gilberto picked up last week looked very similar. If it's your first mask, a unique design might be more fitting?

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: You have an impressive memory.

items.speaker: Young Woman
items.text: I'll be on my way now.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ahem, we spotted a fellow on the street with a mask that caught our eye… I didn't expect it to be Mr. Gilberto from the Fisalia family.

options.text: The Fisalia family?

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Yes, they're one of the oldest families in Rinascita. Their ancestors once ignited a war here, but they were defeated and driven out of Ragunna.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The Fisalias are a mixed lot—while some hold honor, others bring disgrace to the name.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: They show new ambitions. Lately, they've taken to more prominent roles in the city, flaunting their mining ventures and acting with increasing arrogance.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Speaking of which, I revised Monsieur Gilberto's mask twenty-three times…

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I see why it's left a deep impression…

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Anyway, given your standing with the Montellis, I'd advise against this design.

options.text: Why's that?

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Rumor has it the Montellis plan to push for Reserved Echoes at the Carnevale. It's stirred tension with the Fisalia family. Best avoid any association.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: That incident with the Montelli's Echoes going berserk... Unfortunate timing. Who knows how this will affect their promotion.

options.text: I see…

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, the trail leads to the Fisalia family.

options.text: We need to look into the Fisalia family.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The Fisalia's residencies are near the Cathedral of Mercury. We could stop by the Order for more information.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thank you for the lead, Madame Nyarla. Oh, and we will reconsider the mask's design. Thank you for your time.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Hehe, you're too polite, Mademoiselle Zani. You're my special guest, after all.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: Isn't it interesting how a small mask can both hide and reveal? I can't wait to see the "face" you'll choose to show.

items.speaker: Nyarla
items.text: I look forward to your next visit. This splendid reverie is always open for you.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Legends say the Sentinel quelled the storm and stilled the waves, sparing countless lives at sea.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: To show their gratitude, people place candles around the statue, keeping them lit both day and night. Every boat that passes pays their respects.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It may seem fanciful, but it's best to show respect as we pass by.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: So is this the statue of the Sentinel? Why does it feel weird...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We are now in the Fisalia Residencies territory. We should... Wait, look over there…

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: He looks like the man we saw in Mr. Fulmine's memory. Isn't that Gilberto?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's follow him.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Ah, more seekers. What brings you to these halls?

options.text: We're here to visit Sir Gilberto.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Sir Gilberto...? Oh, Gilberto from the Fisalia family.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: A pious soul, frequently here in prayer. But not today, it seems.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: I've heard whispers of you. An Acolyte mentioned your desire to meet the Sentinel. I assume you must be interested in following Their path.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: But I don't think you truly understand the significance of the Carnevale. Allow me to explain.

options.text: I'm interested.
If you must.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: You've probably heard about the connection between the Carnevale and the Sentinel, but do you know why the Carnevale hasn't been held for years?

options.text: I have a general understanding.
Is it because of the accident a decade ago?

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: In the last Carnevale, a sea fog shrouded the entire city. Echoes paraded like lost souls, a dragon roared above, and a beast in the sea stirred up towering waves…

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: It was sent as punishment and warning against our indulgence and division.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: This is our Primus, Fenrico, the Enlightened One.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Amidst that chaos, he stepped forward and quelled the Sentinel's wrath.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Ragunna's greatness was forged from its battles with the sea. It inspired a common goal in all our hearts.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Without everyone working together, land cannot be cultivated, islands cannot unite, and dams cannot be built. A nation's strength lies in its unity.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: You see, this is why we preach restraint and control over our desires. Only through rituals can we strengthen our resolve, our unity!

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: This is why we must banish those who stray from the right path and impose restrictions on those who threaten the common good.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: We've dedicated ourselves to this cause.

options.text: It seems like you disapprove of holding the Carnevale.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Hehe, you misunderstand me.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: I simply mean that the Order's actions are always made with great deliberation.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Guided by the Sentinel, we strive to unite and lead the people. We hope to build a kingdom on high, a land of the blessed… As it was in the beginning, and ever shall be.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: I trust you'll have faith in the Order's judgment. We handle all matters that come to our attention.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: ...What did you say? How could the Montelli family...

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Forgive me, I must attend to pressing matters.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: If you have further questions, seek answers in the cathedral. We are always open to those in search of truth.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: But I remind you to keep your distance from the sacred relics and honor our customs. Otherwise, we will have no choice but to address any act of disrespect.

items.speaker: Timoteo
items.text: Ha, Antonio, surely you know how things work here. It's always about who pays more. Is there a problem?

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Do you Fisalias even honor contracts?

items.speaker: Timoteo
items.text: Ha, since when do Montellis fret over such trivialities?

items.speaker: Timoteo
items.text: Instead of whining, maybe focus on cleaning up the mess your Echoes caused. Ha ha ha...
The Fisalia man walks away, leaving Antonio fuming.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: And you are…?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Greetings, Sir Antonio. I'm Zani from the Averardo Bank.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: This {Male=gentleman;Female=lady} here is a distinguished guest of your family, {PlayerName}. {Male=He;Female=She} was attacked by a rogue Vitreum Dancer while dining, and we're investigating.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Ah, so you're… Please accept my apologies! It's a relief to see you unscathed. We're as puzzled as you are about the Echo's behavior.

options.text: It's okay, but there's something else I want to know.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Miss Carlotta asked me to hold nothing back, so ask away.

options.text: Tell me about Reserved Terminals and Reserved Echoes.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Well, before I do, a little context first.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: In Ragunna, the Order controls all Terminals for Echo use. They store Echoes in their Data Bank and only grant access after thorough screening.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Only a select few Resonators are permitted to carry a Reserved Terminal.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: You see, most Ragunnesi rely on the Genesis Nexus and Common Echoes, with little need for the special access types.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: However, things have changed with Ragunna opening up to the greater Solaris. People want to travel, making Reserved Terminals essential for safety outside the Order's reach.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: You see why demand is up. The Montellis saw an opportunity and started to develop them. After all, necessity is the mother of invention, hm?

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: It should be celebrated, but some think we disrespect the Order's authority or even the Sentinel by doing this. The path forward has been... resistant.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Heh, if you ask me, I suspect the Fisalia family has been spreading slanders about us.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: We back the Carnevale to showcase our Reserved Terminals to the people. It's our chance to prove their worth and attract investors.

options.text: And the Fisalia family has been standing in your way?

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Sigh… The Fisalias have coerced our miners to break their contracts, seizing our largest vein and cutting off our Terminal supply.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: They say the Fisalias have been seen frequenting the Cathedral of Mercury. They must be in league with the Order. How else would they dare to seize our mine so boldly?

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: And it gets worse. With the Carnevale nearing and our Echoes malfunctioning, the family's Executor might have to step in.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: The Carnevale is at risk. While we hope it brings fortune, the festival itself is what truly matters to us, just like the rest of Ragunna.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: We must uncover why the Echo lost control. We cannot let the Fisalias use this to tarnish our reputation any further!

options.text: I can help you with the investigation.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: That would be good for us, but it hardly seems proper to involve our esteemed guest in our troubles...

options.text: I have my own reasons.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Ah, understood. Thank you! But I'm afraid we can't spare any of our own to assist you. Rest assured, the Montellis are always generous. Here's an advance. Once the matter is resolved, you'll be well-rewarded.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Take it. In Ragunna, if you want help, you must offer a reward and one only declines a reward when they refuse to help.

items.speaker: Antonio
items.text: Yes, that's right. Please accept it. If that's all, I must be on my way. There are many matters to attend to...
Antonio departs with a sigh. The weight on his shoulders seems heavier than before.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: From what we know, the Montelli family has no motive for such incidents. They've been anticipating the Carnevale's arrival.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But the Fisalia family is another story. They've been against the celebration from the start, perhaps they could be behind everything? But I don't understand, what could they hope to gain by stopping the Carnevale?

options.text: The mystery continues.
Maybe it's related to the Sentinel?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Why do I feel like I've gotten myself into something above my pay grade…

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You... have a hunch, hm?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: You're right. Let's go.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Looks like they're slowly finding their way.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: No need to rush—I, too, am curious to see what truths await them.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The grand revelry draws closer and closer...
Step forward.
What burdens weigh upon your soul?

options.text: I carry no burdens.
Bless me, for I have transgressed.

items.text: Are you certain? Search your heart.

options.text: Truly, I have nothing to confess.

items.text: What do you wish to confess?

options.text: I sleep too much, eat too much, and squander my days in idleness.
I confess that my remarkable good looks have stirred envy and discord.

items.text: May the Sentinel grant your absolution. Go in peace, and transgress no more.
Now, I shall open the path to your ascension.
Gilberto, how are things progressing?

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Your trust means a lot, Priest Alessio. The "Flower" has been quite... effective.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: A word of caution. Don't overstep. We can't afford unwanted scrutiny.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: I know what I'm doing.

items.speaker: Priest Alessio
items.text: Our sanctuary isn't infinite. Some murmurs are getting harder to quiet.

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: I understand. That's why we seek "unity" and "integration."

items.speaker: Gilberto
items.text: Our beliefs are already one. This is also the destiny They intend.
